title: bromus macrostachys subsp. lanuginosus
intro: Bromus macrostachys subsp. lanuginosus, commonly known as the woolly brome, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The woolly brome has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Consuming the plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and even antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the woolly brome can be made into teas, infusions, or dried extracts, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
